name: Landscaping
title: AI Tools for Landscaping Companies | Turnkey AI
meta_description: Custom AI tools for landscapers — quote new work fast, manage crew dispatch, and keep recurring maintenance contracts on track. Unlimited AI agents for one flat $3,500/month.
hero_lede: Spring buries you in quote requests, crews scatter across town, and the maintenance accounts need attention every week. We build the tools that keep all of it moving.
drains: A spring flood of design and quote requests you can't keep up with
Crews spread across town with no clear daily picture
Recurring maintenance accounts that need steady, reliable contact
The owner checking five tools just to see what the day looks like

tools.title: Quote-request assistant
tools.body: Every design and maintenance inquiry gets an instant reply, the right questions asked, and a site visit booked — so the spring rush turns into a full schedule instead of a backlog.

tools.title: Morning operations brief
tools.body: One text before 8 AM: today's jobs, which crew goes where, callbacks owed, and yesterday's revenue. No more opening five apps in bed to see the day.

tools.title: Maintenance-account assistant
tools.body: It keeps recurring maintenance clients informed — visit reminders, seasonal upsell prompts, renewal nudges — so the contract base stays steady and grows.

tools.title: Review & referral follow-up
tools.body: After a finished install, a personalized message asks happy clients for a review or a referral — the channel that quietly drives most landscaping leads.

cases.context: A 22-crew landscaping company in Central Texas.
cases.before: The owner spent 30+ minutes every morning across five tools before he could see the day.
cases.after: One text at 6 AM: jobs, crew assignments, callbacks, yesterday's revenue.
cases.impact: The owner described it as the single biggest quality-of-life change in five years of running the business.

cases.context: A design-build landscaper overwhelmed each spring.
cases.before: Quote requests piled up faster than the office could respond; many went cold.
cases.after: Every inquiry answered instantly and a site visit booked automatically.
cases.impact: Representative result: a fuller spring schedule with fewer leads lost to slow response.

faq.q: Can it handle both one-off projects and maintenance accounts?
faq.a: Yes. Design-build inquiries get a site visit booked; recurring maintenance accounts get steady contact, visit reminders, and renewal nudges. The tools treat them differently because they are different.

faq.q: Will it work with my scheduling software?
faq.a: Yes. We build on top of LMN, Jobber, Aspire, or whatever you already use to schedule crews.

faq.q: Is the morning brief really just one text?
faq.a: Yes. It pulls from your CRM, scheduling, and accounting tools and sends one clear summary. You read it over coffee instead of opening five apps.
